does: This paper introduces NeuroBreak, a neuron-level analysis system that dissects how large language models respond to jailbreak attacks by examining internal safety mechanisms. The system employs layer-wise representation probing to identify which neurons and layers are responsible for safety decisions and where vulnerabilities exist. By analyzing the internal activation patterns across different layers when processing both benign and adversarial prompts, NeuroBreak reveals how jailbreak techniques bypass safety guardrails at the computational level. The work provides concrete evidence of where and how safety mechanisms fail, enabling systematic diagnosis of vulnerabilities rather than treating model security as a black box.
connects: This research builds on recent efforts to understand and strengthen LLM safety through interpretability techniques. It extends prior work on mechanistic interpretability of neural networks by applying neuron-level analysis specifically to safety-critical behavior. The paper responds to the growing arms race between jailbreak attacks and defensive measures, where adversarial prompt engineering has outpaced our ability to understand why attacks succeed. It incorporates insights from representation learning and probing methods that have been used to characterize what information is encoded at different model layers. By grounding jailbreak analysis in internal model representations, it advances beyond surface-level behavioral testing toward understanding the actual computational mechanisms that enable or fail to enforce safety constraints.
enables: This interpretability-focused approach enables more targeted defenses against jailbreaks by identifying the exact computational pathways that need strengthening. Developers can now selectively reinforce safety mechanisms at vulnerable layers identified through NeuroBreak's analysis, moving beyond generic alignment techniques. The neuron-level insights could guide improved training procedures that more robustly encode safety constraints throughout model depth. Future work can leverage these findings to develop early detection systems that identify jailbreak attempts at the representation level before harmful outputs are generated. Understanding internal mechanisms also supports certification efforts and formal verification approaches, as they provide concrete targets for safety guarantees rather than relying on behavioral testing alone.
